allergies: Nickel (skin rash from some tool belts/jewelry)
health.mental_health: High stress and disrupted sleep related to ongoing conflict with her teenager and repeated calls from school; occasional panic-like episodes when she gets disciplinary emails or phone calls. She has been considering short-term counseling through an employee assistance program but feels embarrassed about it.
recent_visits: Primary care follow-up for A1C and blood pressure check (late January)
Podiatry visit for numbness/tingling in feet and footwear guidance (December)
Urgent care visit for a hand laceration from sheet metal at a job site; tetanus booster updated (November)
Telehealth consult about sleep problems and stress (early February)

finance.debt: Car loan on a 2018 Ford Transit Connect used for commuting and occasional side jobs; credit card balance that grew after paying for tutoring, a private psychological evaluation recommended by school, and replacing a broken phone after her teen threw it during an argument.
investments: Small emergency savings in a high-yield savings account
recent_transactions: Payment to Chicago Public Schools fee portal for missed textbook/device charges and activity fees
Deposit for an educational advocate/IEP consultant consultation
Pharmacy copays for diabetes medications and glucose testing supplies
Payment plan started with a local counseling center for family therapy sessions
Auto insurance premium increase after adding her teen as a permitted driver (despite not being licensed yet)

relationships: Teenage child (ongoing conflicts around school attendance, vaping rumors, and phone use)
Younger sister in Milwaukee who regularly checks in and urges her to pursue counseling and school supports
Ex-partner (child’s other parent) who is inconsistent with visitation and disputes discipline plans
Neighbor in her Albany Park building who sometimes watches her teen after school when Irina is on emergency calls
A close coworker who covers for her when she has to leave early for school meetings
social.social_media_activity: Mostly private Facebook and Instagram accounts; posts photos of Lake Michigan walks and occasional HVAC trade memes. She avoids posting about her teen but has joined a private parent support group for “troubled teens” in Chicago and reads more than she comments.
private_conversations: Texting the school counselor about suspected vaping and skipping classes
Discussing with her sister whether to request a 504 plan/IEP and whether an ADHD evaluation is appropriate
Arguing with her ex-partner about medication compliance and consistent rules across households
Messaging a friend about a recent incident where her teen threatened to run away after being grounded

schedule.work_hours: Typically Monday–Friday 7:00 AM–3:30 PM with rotating on-call evenings and occasional weekend emergency service (more overtime during heat waves and cold snaps).
regular_appointments: Diabetes check-ins with primary care every 3–4 months
Monthly refill pickup for diabetes and blood pressure medications
Standing weekly call with school counselor/assistant principal (usually Thursday afternoons)
upcoming_events: IEP/504 eligibility meeting at the high school (next Tuesday at 10:30 AM)
Endocrinology consult for diabetes medication review (in three weeks)
Court-mandated parent orientation session related to a school incident report (next month)
HVAC union training refresher on refrigerant handling (Saturday in two weeks)
schedule.daily_routine: Up at 5:30 AM to check blood sugar and pack lunch; commutes from the Northwest Side to job sites across the city/suburbs. After work she tries to cook something diabetes-friendly, monitors her teen’s homework/attendance apps, and often fields calls from school or therapy; lights out around 11 PM but sleep is frequently interrupted.

professional.company: Mid-sized union-affiliated mechanical contractor servicing commercial buildings and multi-family properties across Chicago (service and install teams).
projects: Preventive maintenance contract for a River North high-rise (boiler and rooftop units)
Emergency no-heat calls for a property management client in Rogers Park
Replacing VAV boxes and balancing airflow in a West Loop office suite renovation
Spring tune-ups and condenser coil cleaning for multiple condo associations
professional.performance_issues: In the last two months she has had a few late arrivals and had to swap on-call shifts due to school meetings and a suspension-related pickup. Her supervisor has been understanding but asked her to formalize schedule adjustments and stop taking urgent personal calls while on ladders/rooftops for safety.
workplace_relationships: Good rapport with her foreman, who values her troubleshooting skills but is strict about safety and punctuality
A coworker who quietly resents her shift swaps during peak season
Friendly relationship with a building engineer client who requests her specifically for diagnostics

hobbies: Walking the Lakefront Trail when she can
Listening to true-crime and home-improvement podcasts during commutes
DIY apartment fixes (patching drywall, swapping fixtures)
Cooking simple low-carb meals and experimenting with air-fryer recipes
habits: Checks blood glucose most mornings and when she feels shaky on job sites
Keeps protein snacks in her tool bag to avoid hypoglycemia during long calls
Stress-eats late at night during conflicts with her teen (chips/sweets) and feels guilty afterward
Occasional vaping relapse when overwhelmed, hidden from her teen and coworkers
lifestyle.living_situation: Rents a two-bedroom apartment in the Albany Park area; lives with her teenager. The home environment has been tense with frequent arguments about curfew, missing assignments, and phone restrictions, and she sometimes sleeps with her bedroom door locked after blowups for peace of mind.
lifestyle.dietary_preferences: Trying to follow a diabetes-friendly, lower-carb approach (lean proteins, vegetables, fewer sugary drinks). She meal-preps on Sundays when possible but relies on takeout during heavy on-call weeks; she avoids regular soda but struggles with late-night snacking when stressed.
